title: Aureline docs symbol-linked reference packet
description: Boundary schema for the symbol_linked_reference_record frozen in docs/docs_integrity/citation_and_reference_contract.md. A symbol-linked reference is the typed envelope that binds a project symbol, file ref, setting id, command id, or lifecycle id to one or more docs_citation_anchor_record entries (schemas/docs/citation_anchor.schema.json). Docs search result rows, glossary cards, onboarding / guided-tour steps, hover cards, AI-explanation 'cited evidence' rows, hosted-review evidence rows, repair packets, and portability / offboarding exports all reuse this one shape so a reviewer can reconstruct 'which symbol / file / setting the citation bound to, from which pack revision, at which resolution class, under which locale and version-match posture, with which browser-handoff reason if any'. Raw page bodies, raw runbook bodies, raw symbol source, and raw URLs never cross this boundary; the packet carries opaque ids and typed vocabulary only. The eventual docs-integrity crate's Rust types are the schema of record; this file is the cross-tool boundary every non-owning surface reads. Adding a new resolution class, subject-kind, binding-kind, or reuse-surface class is additive-minor and requires a docs_citation_contract_schema_version bump; repurposing an existing value is breaking and requires a new decision row.

docs_citation_contract_schema_version.description: Shared schema version with schemas/docs/citation_anchor.schema.json. Citation-anchor and symbol-linked-reference records evolve lock-step; a bump here MUST bump the sibling schema.

display_label.description: Short human-legible label (e.g. 'workspace_trust::WorkspaceTrustState', 'editor.format_on_save', 'refactor.rename-symbol'). MUST NOT contain raw source bytes, raw comment bodies, raw URLs, or raw file paths; operator-supplied labels for air-gapped origins are admissible. The label is rendered verbatim by docs search, glossary cards, onboarding steps, and support exports.

repair_hook_ref.description: Re-exported from schemas/governance/capability_lifecycle.schema.json without modification (ADR-0011). Required (non-null) when symbol_link_resolution_class is unresolved_requires_refresh or no_claim_yet_support_routed.

subject_kind.description: What kind of product subject this reference binds to. symbol / file / setting / command / capability_lifecycle / keybinding cover the in-product surfaces docs search and the AI-explanation overlay anchor to; runbook_step / release_note / glossary_term / onboarding_step / service_health_event cover the cross-surface reuse cases named in the contract (hosted review, repair packets, guided tours).

binding_kind.description: The kind of docs-side entry the subject binds to. vendor_overlay_inspect_only MUST carry a browser_handoff_reason and packet ref and MUST NOT be projected into AI derived explanations as primary authority. ai_derived_explanation_entry MUST carry at least one citation_anchor_ref whose docs_citation_anchor_record declares derivation = derived or derived_with_upstream_anchors.

symbol_link_resolution_class.description: Re-exported from fixtures/docs/symbol_link_validation_manifest.yaml without modification. Every reference carries exactly one resolution class and, when the resolution traversed fallback rungs, a non-empty symbol_link_resolution_fallback_chain.

reuse_surface.description: Re-exported from schemas/docs/citation_anchor.schema.json without modification. Surfaces that are not named here MUST NOT render this reference as an authoritative row.

derived_explanation_reuse_state.description: Re-exported from fixtures/docs/symbol_link_validation_manifest.yaml without modification. refused_uncited is the typed denial a derived-explanation surface renders when the symbol-linked reference carries a binding_kind = ai_derived_explanation_entry but no upstream citation anchor resolves; silent fallback to a generic answer is forbidden.

subject_ref.description: Opaque pin to the product subject the reference binds to (symbol id, file id, setting id, command id, etc.). Raw source bytes and raw paths never appear here.

symbol_link_resolution_fallback_chain.description: Ordered list of the resolution rungs the resolver traversed before settling on symbol_link_resolution_class. Empty when symbol_link_resolution_class = exact_symbol_match and no fallback was needed. Non-empty when the resolver walked nearby_version_fallback / package_level_guide_fallback / project_docs_outranks_vendor_match / vendor_overrides_project_disclosed / unresolved_requires_refresh / no_claim_yet_support_routed before settling.

citation_anchor_refs.description: Docs_citation_anchor_record anchor_ids backing this reference. MUST be non-empty when binding_kind = ai_derived_explanation_entry or when derived_explanation_reuse_state = reusable_with_citation_anchor. MAY be empty for unresolved_requires_refresh / no_claim_yet_support_routed resolution classes; in that case, repair_hook_ref MUST be non-null.

browser_handoff_reason.description: Required (non-null) when the reference resolves via an out-of-product browser handoff (vendor_overlay_inspect_only, mirrored portal, external_status_feed).

repair_hook_ref.description: Required (non-null) when symbol_link_resolution_class is unresolved_requires_refresh or no_claim_yet_support_routed, or when derived_explanation_reuse_state is any of the refused_* values.

superseded_by_reference_id.description: Opaque pin to a later symbol-linked-reference that supersedes this one after a pack revision bump, rename-refactor, or symbol removal. Surfaces render the typed 'superseded' disclosure and route to migrate_to_replacement when set.

allOf.description: browser_handoff_packet_ref MUST be non-null when browser_handoff_reason is non-null.
repair_hook_ref MUST be non-null when symbol_link_resolution_class is unresolved_requires_refresh or no_claim_yet_support_routed.
binding_kind = vendor_overlay_inspect_only MUST carry a browser_handoff_reason + packet ref.
binding_kind = ai_derived_explanation_entry MUST carry at least one citation_anchor_ref.
derived_explanation_reuse_state = reusable_with_citation_anchor MUST carry at least one citation_anchor_ref.

symbol_linked_reference_record.description: Symbol-linked reference packet binding a product subject (symbol, file, setting, command, capability lifecycle, keybinding, runbook step, release note, glossary term, onboarding step, or service-health event) to one or more docs_citation_anchor_record entries. Docs search, glossary cards, guided tours, hover cards, AI-explanation overlays, hosted-review evidence, repair packets, and portability / offboarding exports reuse this one record so the 'what symbol / file the citation bound to, from which pack revision, at which resolution class, under which locale, with which browser-handoff reason if any' chain survives export and support handoff without flattening into prose-only authority.
